# Beudox HR Portal - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

This is a modern React TypeScript frontend application built with Vite, serving as the user interface for a comprehensive HR management system. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Key Architectural Decisions

- **Component Organization**: Components are organized by feature domains (attendance, leave, payroll, etc.) with shared UI components in a separate directory
- **State Management**: Uses React hooks and context for local state, with Supabase for server state and real-time updates
- **Routing**: Client-side routing with React Router, supporting nested routes and protected routes
- **Styling**: Utility-first CSS with Tailwind, custom design tokens for consistent branding
- **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript coverage with strict type checking

## Core Application Structure

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap with React 18, router setup, and global styles
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main dashboard/home page component

### Key Directories
- `src/components/`: Feature-specific and reusable UI components
- `src/lib/`: Utility functions, formatters, and business logic helpers
- `src/hooks/`: Custom React hooks for data fetching and state management
- `src/integrations/`: External service integrations (primarily Supabase)

## Component Architecture

### Layout Components
- `AppLayout`: Main application shell with responsive sidebar navigation
- `AppSidebar`: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- `TopBar`: Header with user menu, notifications, and search
- `MobileSidebarContext`: Context for mobile sidebar state management

### Feature Components

#### Attendance
- `AttendanceSummary`: Complex analytics component displaying attendance metrics, overtime insights, and anomaly detection
- `AttendanceUploadFlow`: File upload interface for bulk attendance data import
- `MissingEntryModal`: Modal for handling incomplete attendance records

#### Employee Profile
- Multiple tab components (AttendanceTab, LeaveTab, PayrollTab, etc.) for employee profile sections
- `PendingIncrementCard`: Displays pending salary increment proposals
- `ProposeIncrementModal`: Modal for HR to propose salary changes
- `ReviewScheduleSection`: Component for managing employee review schedules

#### Leave Management
- `AllRequestsTab`: HR view of all leave requests
- `ApplyLeaveModal`: Employee interface for submitting leave requests
- `LeaveBalancesTab`: Display of leave balances and history
- `MyLeaveBalances`: Employee's personal leave balance overview

#### Payroll
- `PayrollSummary`: Payroll data aggregation and display
- `PayrollDetailSheet`: Detailed payroll calculation views
- `PayslipCard`: Individual payslip display component

#### Settings (Admin)
- Multiple configuration tabs for company settings, departments, roles, leave types, etc.
- `RichTextEditor`: WYSIWYG editor for HR policy documents

### UI Component Library
- Extensive use of shadcn/ui components (buttons, forms, tables, dialogs, etc.)
- Custom components like `SearchableEmployeeSelect` for employee selection with search
- `BeudoxLogo` component with multiple display variants

## Data Flow & Business Logic

### Authentication Flow
- Supabase authentication with custom user management
- `MandatoryPasswordChange` component enforces password updates for new users
- Role-based access control throughout the application

### Attendance Processing
- Records stored with timestamps, working hours calculation, overtime tracking
- Analytics include attendance rates, late arrival patterns, weekend work detection
- Anomaly detection for frequent absences and incomplete records

### Leave Management
- Balance tracking with automatic calculations
- Approval workflows with status tracking
- Integration with attendance for leave day counting

### Payroll Processing
- Complex calculations including regular/holiday overtime
- Integration with attendance data for accurate hour tracking
- PDF generation for payslips

## Key Utility Functions

### Formatting & Display
- `formatDate`, `formatTime12h`: Date/time formatting utilities
- `formatWorkingHours`: Attendance hour display formatting
- `cn`: Class name utility for conditional styling

### Business Logic
- `countWorkingDays`: Working day calculations excluding holidays
- `ensureLeaveBalance`: Leave balance maintenance
- `computeNextReviewDate`: Review schedule calculations
- `hasRole`, `canAccess`: Role-based permission checking

### Data Processing
- `login-tracking.ts`: Device and location tracking for security
- `client-activity.ts`: Activity logging and categorization
- `notifications.ts`: In-app notification system

## Database Integration

### Supabase Schema (Frontend Perspective)
- `employees`: User profiles with role and company associations
- `attendance_records`: Time tracking data with calculated fields
- `leave_requests`: Leave application and approval data
- `payroll_records`: Salary and overtime calculations
- `public_holidays`: Company holiday configurations
- `evaluations`: Performance review data
- `projects`: Project management with team assignments

### Real-time Features
- Live updates for attendance changes
- Notification system for approvals and updates
- Activity logging for audit trails

## Configuration & Environment

### Build Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite configuration with React plugin
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind with custom color scheme
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration with strict settings

### Environment Variables
- Supabase project configuration (URL, keys)
- Build-time environment variables prefixed with `VITE_`

## Testing Strategy

- Unit tests with Vitest for utility functions and hooks
- E2E tests with Playwright for critical user flows
- Component testing focused on business logic rather than UI styling

## Performance Considerations

- Lazy loading of route components
- Optimized re-renders with React.memo where appropriate
- Efficient data fetching with Supabase RLS policies
- Minimal bundle size through tree shaking and code splitting

## Security Features

- Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) for data access control
- Client-side route protection based on user roles
- Secure password policies enforced in `MandatoryPasswordChange`
- Login tracking for security monitoring
- Input validation and sanitization throughout forms

This application represents a production-ready HR management system with enterprise-level features, security, and scalability considerations.
